sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 5

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 5:1
items.html: (to the question) Just as a connection is needed by which the upper one can raise to itself the MAN [Mayin Nukvin] of its lower one — namely through the Kli [vessel] that raises MAN, as above — so the lower one needs a connection by which it can rise as MAN to its upper one, after it already has the reality of Mochin of Yenika [suckling]. For the lower one cannot ascend from its place so much as a hair's breadth; how then can it rise to its upper one in the manner of MAN? The matter is that the very Kli and Rucha [spirit] that are the intermediary for the upper one to raise the lower one's MAN also serve when it has been completed in Mochin of Yenika, so that it can rise there, to the place of its Rucha that remained in the upper one, in the discernment of firstborn sons that are never missing from there. For by the power of the first MAN — see nearby, in Answer 109 — with which every lower one illuminated in its upper-upper, every lower one that has been completed in Mochin of Yenika can again raise MAN to its upper one, and raise it, for its own need, to the upper-upper; since from the relation of that first MAN they are in the discernment of actual brothers, as above. Hence, from that relation, every lower one stands with its upper one in the discernment of an equal degree, both coming from one cause, the upper-upper, and in that relation the upper one has no superiority of being higher than the lower one. Therefore, from the discernment of that Rucha, the lower one can rise to the upper one. And thus that Rucha became the discernment of a root for all the MAN in them all, for there is no raising of MAN that does not cause a Zivug [coupling] in all the degrees, all the way to Ein Sof, blessed be He, since no renewal of Lights emerges except from Ein Sof, as is known. Hence that Rucha Kadma'a [first spirit] is what always brings, in each and every MAN, the lower one to the upper one; and in this, every discernment of Rucha Kadma'a of all the Partzufim is considered the discernment of a root for all the kinds of MAN below it — understand. (Item 8)
